stories.title: Lynx star Kayla McBride becomes latest to speak out on WNBA 3-point contest drama: ‘Half a-- invite’
stories.summary: WNBA star Kayla McBride has spoken out about her absence from the league's 3-point contest, claiming she received a "half-ass invite" through her agent just 48 hours before the event. The Minnesota Lynx guard joins New York Liberty star Sabrina Ionescu in criticizing the WNBA's communication, with both players saying they were not officially invited to participate. The controversy comes as the league prepares for its 2026 All-Star game.

stories.title: Scientists warn of potential global marine invasive species event linked to idle ships in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Thousands of commercial vessels stranded in the Persian Gulf for months following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz due to fighting between the US and Iran may unintentionally trigger a massive global marine invasive species event when they resume normal trade routes. The prolonged periods at anchor have allowed ship hulls to accumulate dense communities of marine organisms, including algae, barnacles, and mussels, which can be transported around the world. This could lead to a global marine bioinvasion "super-spreader" event, with potentially devastating consequences for ecosystems worldwide.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Russia's largest online retailer, Wildberries, has seen four of its warehouses targeted by Ukrainian drones in recent days, including two in the Moscow region and two in the Tambov and Krasnodar regions, resulting in significant losses and disruptions to supplies to the Russian army and hundreds of small businesses that rely on the online marketplace. The attacks have killed at least eight people and caused millions of dollars in inventory to be destroyed, sparking an online outcry among Russian small-business owners. The strikes are part of Ukraine's campaign of economic warfare against Russia, which has also included drone attacks on oil refineries and depots.

stories.title: My Word: Political name of the game
stories.summary: Israel’s political parties are reshuffling names and merging ahead of the October 27 election, with many now known by their leaders’ nicknames like Bibi or Eisenkot. Analysts say generic party names and frequent shifts make it hard for voters to track alliances. The latest round of changes could reshape the race just weeks before ballots are cast.

stories.title: Tyla – ‘A*Pop’ review: safe, polished pop that could’ve gone further
stories.summary: South African singer Tyla released her second album, APop, on May 17, following her 2023 global hit Water. The album blends R&B and amapiano but leans heavily on slow-burning love songs, leaving critics underwhelmed. Tyla’s next single is expected in early June.

stories.title: 2026 WNBA 3-Point Contest Odds, Prediction, Participants, Past Winners (Bet on Rhyne Howard, Fade Azzi Fudd)
stories.summary: The 2026 WNBA 3-Point Contest kicks off Friday night at Chicago’s Wintrust Arena with six sharpshooters vying for the title. Atlanta’s Rhyne Howard, a 2022 finalist, leads the field, while Azzi Fudd of Dallas is the betting favorite at +210. The event, starting July 24, marks the first contest since 2024 with no returning finalists.

stories.title: I spent years trying to get smaller. Until I decided to get bigger
stories.summary: A woman who had been lifting weights for years fell into an unhealthy body image spiral after planning her wedding, leading her to obsess over her weight and restrict her food intake. She began weighing herself daily and tracking her calorie and step counts, eventually resorting to binge eating when she couldn't cope with her restrictive diet. This pattern of behavior has been linked to a higher risk of eating disorders and other mental health issues.

stories.title: Guardiola turns down Italy manager job
stories.summary: Pep Guardiola has turned down the opportunity to coach the Italian national team, despite being offered a significant salary and the chance to shape the job as he saw fit. The Italian Football Federation had approached Guardiola after he left Manchester City this summer, but he has decided to take a break from the game to rest and spend more time with his family. Former Italy playmaker Andrea Pirlo is now the frontrunner to take over the team.

stories.title: UK mortgage rates rise to highest level for a month
stories.summary: UK mortgage rates have risen to their highest level in a month, with 100 deals being temporarily pulled by lenders as they reassess their pricing plans. This sudden increase in rates has left borrowers feeling frustrated and uncertain, with experts warning that the market needs stability to recover. As a result, borrowers are advised to lock in new deals with their existing lenders or seek help from a broker to explore better options.

stories.title: Microsoft pressures LG into killing unwanted McAfee ads
stories.summary: Microsoft has successfully pressured LG into disabling unwanted McAfee ads on Windows 11 PCs connected to LG monitors, following a year-long backlash against large pop-up ads for McAfee antivirus and LG's own apps. The move comes after Microsoft's Windows chief, Pavan Davuluri, intervened to address user complaints about the LG bloatware. LG has agreed to immediately disable the McAfee pop-up from its LG Monitor App Installer, ending a contentious issue that began with reports of auto-installing driver updates in 2024.

stories.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
stories.summary: French authorities have ordered the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula, with over 40,000 people fleeing by boat as wildfires in the Gironde region have consumed 8,700 hectares of land since Tuesday. The fires have destroyed around 80 homes, with 50 completely destroyed, and firefighters continue to battle the blaze. The evacuation is part of a larger effort to contain the wildfires, which have also prompted a national emergency declaration in Spain.

stories.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
stories.summary: US and Iranian forces have engaged in a new round of airstrikes in the Middle East, with Iran targeting US military facilities and bases for the 13th consecutive night in response to American attacks. The strikes resulted in four deaths and five injuries in Iran's south-west, and also hit US-allied Kuwait, prompting flight suspensions at a major Iraqi airport. The escalation comes as the US plans to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages, a move Iran has condemned as a dangerous precedent.

stories.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, and the European Union, with a 10% to 12.5% tariff on all goods, accounting for almost all American imports. The move is reportedly aimed at tackling forced labour, although US trade experts claim it is a pretext for Trump to maintain tariffs. The new duties will take effect immediately, replacing an identical levy that expires on Friday.

stories.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
stories.summary: Angela Rayner, the Housing Secretary, has ruled out introducing rent controls in England, citing the impact of the Renters' Rights Act which came into effect in May. The Act has already had a significant effect on the market, according to Rayner, and she believes that controls implemented in some areas of the UK have not necessarily led to lower rents. This decision comes as Scotland prepares to introduce new rent control measures by 2027.

stories.title: Jon Bon Jovi Ends Madison Square Garden Show Early Due to Sinus Infection: ‘We’ll Figure Out How to Reschedule’
stories.summary: Rock legend Jon Bon Jovi abruptly ended his Madison Square Garden show on Thursday night due to a sinus infection, forcing the cancellation of the remaining 90 minutes of the performance. The frontman, who has been enjoying the band's residency at the iconic venue, reassured fans that they would be able to reschedule the show and encouraged them to hold onto their ticket stubs. A representative for the band confirmed that Bon Jovi has been battling the infection and will provide updated information on rescheduling the show in the coming days.

stories.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
stories.summary: A South Korean court has ordered SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won to pay his ex-wife Roh Soh-yeong $644 million in a divorce settlement, a significant reduction from the initial $1.38 trillion award. The couple, married for 35 years, had been embroiled in a high-profile divorce case following Chey's admission of fathering a child with another woman. The verdict marks a major conclusion to the long-running "divorce of the century" case.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: A devastating 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years has left nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-olds without work, education, or training, with many young people in coastal towns feeling isolated and despondent due to a lack of opportunities. This alarming trend has been exacerbated by negative media portrayals of young people in these areas, often described as "lazy" or "feral". The Against the Tide project will continue to report on the lives of young people in coastal communities across England and Wales over the next year.

stories.title: As Canada Opens Gordie Howe International Bridge, Americans Are Told to Stay Home
stories.summary: After a delay ordered by President Trump and new tariffs against Canada, the bridge between Windsor, Ontario, and Detroit opens in “underwhelming” fashion on Friday.

stories.title: Democrats Prepare for Possible Election Clashes With Federal Government
stories.summary: Top officials overseeing state elections and law enforcement are stepping up plans to prepare for what they worry could be interference in the midterms.

stories.title: Iran War and Trump’s Tariffs Threaten a Resilient US Economy
stories.summary: As renewed fighting with Iran sends oil and gas prices soaring, President Trump has imposed new global tariffs that could push up prices further.

stories.title: ‘Jumbo’ Sequel, ‘Queen of Malacca’ Franchise Plans on the Cards as Indonesia’s Visinema Eyes Expansion (EXCLUSIVE)
stories.summary: Indonesian filmmaker Angga Dwimas Sasongko is planning to release the fantasy epic "Queen of Malacca" in the first quarter of 2027, following significant industry attention and interest from potential distribution partners in China, Thailand, and other international markets. The film is currently in post-production and has attracted attention from various countries, indicating a potential expansion of Visinema's global reach. This move is part of the company's plans to increase its international presence.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
stories.summary: India's government has agreed to hold talks with protesters from the Cockroach Janta Party on Friday, as thousands continue to demand the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over examination paper leaks. The talks come after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike in solidarity with the protests, and Prime Minister Narendra Modi promised a new law to tackle the issue. The protests, involving tens of thousands of young people, have become a major challenge for Modi's government.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron has been spotted at the National Trust's Holnicote Estate in Somerset, England, marking a significant sighting in the UK where this species is rarely seen. The heron's appearance is attributed to the estate's efforts to restore freshwater habitats, which are also providing a refuge for other wildlife including water voles, otters, and white-tailed eagles. This conservation success highlights the importance of preserving and renewing wetlands to improve water quality, reduce flood risk, and create more resilient landscapes.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A new play area at Winchester Science Centre in Hampshire aims to educate children about England's unique chalk streams, with over 80 percent of the world's chalk streams found in the country. The prolonged hot and dry weather poses a significant threat to the wildlife that inhabits these rare waterways. The centre's interactive exhibit is designed to inspire a lifelong connection with the environment in children, who will learn about the importance of water flow, temperature, and oxygen for the chalk streams' survival.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found that replacing gas stoves with electric ones can lead to a significant reduction in asthma symptoms, with benefits comparable to those of commonly used asthma medications. The study, which involved 72 households in Ohio, showed a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school. Further research is now needed to confirm these findings and explore their potential to improve the lives of millions of people worldwide.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.
